title: pauridiantha paucinervis subsp. lyallii
intro: Pauridiantha paucinervis subsp. lyallii is a plant species, also known as the lyall's pauridiantha, which is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its bark and roots, which are used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pauridiantha paucinervis subsp. lyallii include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Pauridiantha paucinervis subsp. lyallii are typically made by infusing the bark and roots in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
